decisions.relationship_evidence: The title reports latent class analysis, and the study analyzes symptom data across nine databases to identify post-COVID subclasses.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to post-acute COVID-19 symptom heterogeneity.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The title explicitly identifies the record as a correction to a latent class analysis paper.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Formal correction of the named latent class analysis article.
named_source_mentions: Vaccine Hesitancy or Hesitancies? A Latent Class Analysis of Pediatric Patients' Parents

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors used latent class analysis on nine survey items to estimate high- versus low-hesitancy class membership.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to COVID-19 vaccine hesitancy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis partitioned sexual partnerships into four typologies subsequently related to HIV seropositivity.
A trust typology was derived from latent class analysis of six trusted-information indicators.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to HPV and HPV-vaccine awareness.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis identified four participant subgroups used to analyze PrEP initiation.
The study used latent class analysis to cluster transgender women by HIV risk behaviors and relate classes to PrEP willingness.
Latent class analysis of 12 knowledge, attitude, and belief items identified vaccine questioner and acceptor archetypes.
Latent class analysis identified socioeconomic-position groups subsequently compared on PrEP access and use.
Longitudinal social-determinant measures were modeled with latent class analysis, producing a two-class solution related to vaccination timing.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete COVID-19 vaccination application among women with HIV.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis divided participants into mutually exclusive risk and service-access classes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV and hepatitis C risk application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis was used on clinical data to identify five bacteremia subphenotypes across three cohorts.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to Staphylococcus aureus bacteremia heterogeneity.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis estimated the sensitivity and specificity of four pathogen-detection tests without asserting a perfect reference.
Latent class analysis segmented children using parental vaccination intent, attitudes, and behavior.
Latent class analysis identified four sexual-behavior profiles and related them to HIV testing and PrEP uptake.
Latent class analysis of antimicrobial susceptibility data identified four latent resistance phenotypes.
Mixture regression analysis identified two latent psychosocial-behavioral classes with different predictors of preventive intentions.
Latent class analysis identified four behavioral-cognitive HIV-risk subgroups and related them to PrEP and PEP use.
Latent class analysis identified four HIV-prevention life-skill classes and their associated factors.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV-prevention application among university students.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis grouped participants by socio-ecological vulnerabilities and related class membership to adherence and treatment outcomes.
Latent class analysis classified 13 prevention indicators into four behavioral patterns and compared their HIV positivity.
Latent class analysis identified three Slovak population segments differing in vaccination beliefs and behavior.
Latent class analysis identified three motivational classes among COVID-19 vaccine-hesitant and vaccine-refusing respondents.
Latent class analysis of reported sexual behaviors identified three HIV-risk classes among college students.
Multidimensional latent class analyses identified four combinations of perceived community stigmas.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application involving HIV stigma and testing outcomes.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Person-centered latent class analysis identified two HBV knowledge and misconception profiles.
Panel latent class regression models analyzed heterogeneity in pandemic-vaccination preferences from a discrete-choice experiment.
A latent class model analyzed discrete-choice data and identified two groups with different HIV-testing preferences.
Latent class analysis examined preference heterogeneity in a PrEP discrete-choice experiment and identified three preference classes.
A latent class multinomial logit model identified five patterns of SARS-CoV-2 testing preferences from discrete-choice data.
Latent class analysis and multinomial latent class regression identified three patterns of interest in novel PrEP formulations.
Latent class analysis grouped respondents by health-decision patterns and related classes to ranked PrEP modality preferences.
Latent class analysis identified three groups based on preferences for four PrEP attributes in a discrete-choice experiment.
Latent class analysis of a discrete-choice experiment identified three preference patterns for HIV care-coordination features.
Latent class analysis segmented discrete-choice respondents according to preferences for long-acting injectable PrEP attributes.
Latent class analysis of discrete-choice data identified three preference subgroups for infant RSV preventives.
A latent class logit model analyzed preference heterogeneity for newly developed and established vaccines.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete COVID-19 and influenza vaccine-choice application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The title and abstract explicitly identify a corrigendum correcting the latent class logit study.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Formal correction of the named preference-model article.
named_source_mentions: Investigating the influencing factors of vaccination decisions for newly-developed and established vaccines: a comparative study based on latent class logit models in China

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis identified three groups from reported PrEP modality preferences and examined class predictors.
A latent-class model analyzed discrete-choice data and identified four HIV-service preference groups.
Latent class analysis of repeated adherence measurements identified three long-term adherence trajectories.
The article develops a Bayesian joint model linking finite-mixture longitudinal trajectories and time-to-event outcomes through latent class indicators.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological-development paper with simulations and an AIDS-data illustration.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis and growth mixture modeling estimated repeated trajectories of peer norms, self-efficacy, and condom use.
Group-based multi-trajectory analysis identified six longitudinal adherence and retention patterns and related them to mortality.
A latent class mixed model represented heterogeneous longitudinal CD4, CD8, and HIV-load dynamics.
A latent class growth model separated HIV-testing self-efficacy trajectories into intervention-response and nonresponse classes.
A latent class-informed regression analysis identified four post-consultation respiratory-infection symptom trajectories.
Latent class mixture models identified four longitudinal PrEP continuation and coverage trajectories.
Latent class analysis of four longitudinal survey waves identified changing COVID-19 preventive-behavior patterns.
The review summarizes applications, advantages, and limitations of latent class growth models in HIV prevention and control.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological review and guidance source for infectious-disease applications.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis of adverse events across vaccine doses identified two longitudinal susceptibility groups.
The commentary directly discusses the consequences of measurement error and misclassification and evidence that improved measurements altered epidemiologic effect estimates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Method-focused commentary on measurement and misclassification in HPV epidemiology.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors propose normalized absorbance ratios as a standardization procedure to control assay variation and reduce measurement error.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development and empirical evaluation of a measurement-error reduction procedure.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper proposes normalization, comparison, and sample-size methods for combining laboratory data while adjusting inter-source measurement error.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological-development paper supported by simulation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Comparison with a single-copy assay quantifies underreporting and error by the Amplicor assay near its detection limit.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit evaluation of infectious-disease assay measurement error.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The article presents an extended longitudinal modeling approach for adjusting baseline measurement error in time-varying covariates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit extension of a measurement-error adjustment method.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study accounts for replicate-measurement error when estimating the effect of sample holding time on Legionella counts.
The authors develop a Bayesian model combining registry and seroprevalence data to adjust vaccine coverage for registry underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit method development illustrated with meningococcal vaccination data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Multiple overimputation was used to handle both missingness and measurement error in CD4 and viral-load data, with performance assessed by simulation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV-data application of an existing correction method.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study demonstrates multiple overimputation for jointly addressing missing gestational-age data and measurement error.
Bayesian reclassification was used to correct pertussis vaccine-effectiveness estimates for case misclassification and underreporting.
The authors develop the underreporting correction factor, its confidence intervals, and sensitivity analyses for correcting self-reported risk prevalence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a biomarker-based misreporting correction method.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study estimates measurement error and excess random noise for three HIV RNA assays near their quantification limits.
The paper proposes a Bayesian method for joint longitudinal competing-risk models with covariate measurement error.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development validated by simulation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors propose an extension of simulation-extrapolation for non-zero-mean measurement errors depending on error-free covariates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit measurement-error correction development with simulations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper develops, validates, and publicly provides the hivmmer pipeline for reducing erroneous HIV sequence variant calls.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit software implementation addressing sequencing measurement error.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulation and an Ecuadorian case study quantify effect-estimate bias caused by spatiotemporal rainfall exposure misclassification.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit performance and bias evaluation of exposure measurement error.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The article presents fully Bayesian semiparametric mixed-effects models for predictors with measurement error and missing values.
The paper proposes estimating-equation and imputation methods for interval-censored transformation models with covariate measurement error.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development evaluated by simulation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors introduce an estimator of natural indirect effects robust to mediator measurement error and specified unmeasured confounding.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit estimator development with proofs and simulation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The article extends cumulative-incidence estimation to differential outcome misclassification using external validation data and bootstrap uncertainty propagation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological extension with consistency results and simulations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study performs proportional redistribution of ill-defined causes of death to correct underreported or nonspecific cause categories.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete burden-estimation application that includes lower respiratory infections.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study benchmarks metagenome-inference methods and demonstrates differential measurement error and resulting bias across vaginal community types.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit evaluation of measurement-error performance in microbiome inference.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors propose a nonparametric pseudo-likelihood method and EM-like algorithm for semi-competing risks with event misascertainment.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit correction-method development with simulation and HIV application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper derives three corrected-score estimators that jointly address confounding and classical exposure measurement error.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit estimator development with theory, simulation, and software.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors introduce a control-variates framework for flexible causal estimation with error-prone exposures and validation data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development with theoretical properties and simulations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper extends the Rogan-Gladen estimator to risk and survival functions subject to outcome measurement error and censoring.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit correction-method extension supported by simulations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors introduce an additional variance parameter for nonsampling errors in antenatal-clinic HIV surveillance and demonstrate improved prediction coverage.
Bayesian adjustment was applied to examine how HBV underreporting and misclassification changed vaccination odds-ratio estimates.
Existing Bayesian methods adjusted COVID-19 surveillance curves for testing misclassification and infection-date uncertainty before estimating reproduction numbers.
The report attributes delayed SARS-CoV-2 testing rollout to primer and probe batches contaminated with synthetic control material.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct diagnosis of synthetic-control contamination in molecular diagnostics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study triangulates and compares face-to-face interviews, computer-assisted self-interviews, and prospective diaries for measuring sexual behavior.
supported_label_variants: triangulation of three data collection instruments

decisions.relationship_evidence: Three partially overlapping databases were analyzed with capture-recapture and log-linear regression to estimate unobserved HIV-positive pregnancies.
A three-source capture-recapture log-linear model estimated HIV-positive individuals absent from all registries.
Linked records from three HIV registers were analyzed through log-linear capture-recapture estimation, with additional mortality-underreporting adjustment.
A multi-event capture-recapture procedure quantified survival, immunity transitions, and infection dynamics in rabbit populations.

The paper uses capture-recapture data within a broader Bayesian population-and-disease model, but the title and abstract do not explicitly support a multistate or multi-event capture-recapture method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit exact-concept lineage lead is stated.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Multievent capture-recapture models estimated survival, recapture, serostatus transitions, and lyssavirus dynamics in bats.
A multi-event capture-recapture model explicitly accounted for uncertainty in cat age and serostatus while estimating Toxoplasma seroconversion.
Simulation experiments evaluate the bias and precision of multi-event capture-mark-recapture estimates under increasing infection-state uncertainty.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct performance and robustness evaluation of the exact method.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulations compare cross-sectional and longitudinal capture-recapture designs for estimating force of infection and evaluate integrated-design performance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Method-focused evaluation of capture-recapture designs for disease dynamics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Multi-event capture-recapture models combined molecular and serological data to estimate prevalence and diagnostic performance for sarcoptic mange.
The study directly tests whether multi-event capture-mark-recapture models can reliably estimate serological-test sensitivity and specificity without reference samples.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct methodological performance evaluation against finite-mixture estimates.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors develop a susceptible-infectious-recovered multi-event capture-recapture model and MCMC algorithm for estimating undetected COVID-19 cases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development and validation of a dynamic multi-event capture-recapture model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Artificial neural networks were trained, tested, and validated to predict three neonatal outcomes and evaluate palivizumab effects during RSV outbreaks.
A back-propagation artificial neural network was fitted and evaluated for daily infectious-diarrhea incidence forecasting.
The study benchmarks generalized-regression neural-network, ARIMA, and hybrid models using multiple forecasting-error measures.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct comparative performance evaluation for hemorrhagic-fever forecasting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper compares BP artificial-neural-network and ARIMA fitting and forecasting performance for monthly AIDS incidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct model-performance comparison in HIV/AIDS forecasting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors propose and evaluate a hybrid neural network combining attention-based bidirectional LSTM and autoencoder components for blood-culture prediction.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit diagnostic-model development and comparative evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper proposes and tests an interpretable deep convolutional neural network that diagnoses latent CMV directly from cytometry data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit diagnostic neural-network development with a public implementation tutorial.
